generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T HYDROPHOBINUM. The virus from the mad dog. Preparation , Trituration with sugar of milk. Authorities. ( 1 to 7 , provings by J. R. Coxe, Jr., M.D.; Phil. J. of Hom., 3, p. 262). 1 , J. R. C., Jr., took 3d dil. three times a day for four days; 1 a , proving with 6th dil.; 1 b , proving with 30th dil.; 1 c , proving with 3d dec. dil.; 2 , Daniel Coxe, aged 17, took 3d dil. in same manner; 2 a , same took 6th dil.; 3 , Charles Coxe, aged 7, took 6th dil.; 4 , N. E. W., aged 18, took 3d dil.; 4 a , same took 6th dil.; 5 , same took 30th dil.; 6 , A. R. S., aged 18, took 3d dil.; 6 a , same took 6th dil.; 7 , Miss A. S., aged 30, took 3d dil.; 7 a , same took 6th dil.; 7 b , same took 30th dil.; 7 c , same took 3d dec. dil.; ( 8 to 18 , and a few authorities given after the symptoms, from same reference, no details furnished); 8 , Hering; 9 , "B.," Behlert on females.; 10 , N., Neidhard; 11 , "V.;" [erase "11 v. (vide reference to other symptoms. -Hering.)] 12 , "Schm.;" 13 , "W.;" 14 , "My.;" 15 , "E.;" 16 , "Br.;" 17 , "C.;" 18 , Peterson. MIND. ► Emotional. ► Strange notions and apprehensions during pregnancy, [8] . ► Exaltation; thinks he has something of importance, [8] . ► Singing, in walking through the whole house, entire day, [11] . ► Sang more than usual, but did not feel at all merry; singing involuntary, [7] . ► Happy disposition, after perspiring in the evening, [11] . ► Exhilarated, felt as if he had received joyful intelligence, all day, [4] . ► During the first two or three days, temper more equable than usual, [7] . ► Felt unusually serious, [7] . ► Had a good cry before going to bed; felt very sad (third day), [7] . [10.] ► Bitterly crying, with headache, [11] . ► Anxiety of mind, restlessness, with great prostration, [11] ; with pain in the heart, [11] ; with the headache, [13] . ► Feeling as if I had heard, or was about to hear something bad; morose and crabbed feelings until 4 P.M. (fourth day), [1a] . ► Mind much depressed; felt as if something disagreeable was about to happen (third day), [3] . ► Feeling as if something annoying was about to happen; goes off when thinking of it, [1] . ► He cannot prevent ideas of something awful about to happen, or as if he would do something awful, [8] . ► An indescribable idea, which I could not shake off, of something dreadful about to happen to me; all day feeling as if some great misfortune were about to occur, [1c] . ► Irritability, [9] . ► Irritable, with headache, [11] . ► Feel irritable; spoke short to the children (ninth day), [7] . [20.] ► Very irritable at trifles, [7b] . ► Am extremely irritable, or rather feel stern (sixth day), [7b] . ► Disposed to get mad, [10] . ► Impatient all day, [7a] . ► Impatient all day, with headache, [11] . ► Cross and hypochondriac in the evening, [12] . ► Felt very cross; did not want to converse or see any one, [1c] . ► Very cross and crabbed until 10.30 P.M., when I fell asleep, [1c] . ► Very cross, so much so that my children expressed great surprise; took offence at the merest trifles; scolded my wife and children; could not concentrate my mind of anything, [1c] . ► The last day or two feel more morose; caught myself saying to nephew, in severe tone, "If you do that again," etc., when he was not really in fault, [1] . [30.] ► Offended at everything; gives offensive answers, [9] . ► Inclined to use offensive expressions, [8] . ► Thought came into his mind to attack others in a mean way; to cut others with a knife he holds; to throw water he has in a tumbler into another's face, [8] . ► Occasionally exhilarated, then again morose, both feelings going off very readily on conversing, [1] . ► Intellectual. ► There seems to be two distinct trains of thought operating at one and the same time, [7b] . ► Plays chess much better, but cannot relate a story, [8] . ► Undecided about little things, [8] . ► Have found it difficult through the whole proving to think intently, and at times almost impossible (seventh day), [7] . ► Restlessness in the mind during the forenoon and at noon, [12] . ► A strange oppression and indifference, as if he was unable to do anything if he forces himself; there is no power in the mind, [8] . [40.] ► Dulness and stupidity (second day); at night restlessness, [15] . ► Memory for words much better, [10] . ► He loses consciousness for a moment, [8] . HEAD. ► Vertigo. ► Vertigo while sitting, and after rising or walking he staggers, [9] . ► Slight vertigo when rising from a chair (sixth day), [2] . ► Dizzy, as if he could not hold the head, [8] . ► After stooping dizzy, and losing sight, [9] . ► Dizzy headache (eleventh day), [7] . ► Dizzy headache all day, spirits not depressed (third day), [7b] . ► A dizzy dulness in the vertex, he fears he will fall while walking, in the evening, [8] . [50.] ► Slight dizziness and nausea (sixth day), [7] . ► While stooping, giddiness on the right, [11] . ► After lying down in bed, a giddy-like shock in the upper part of brain, [8] . ► General Head. ► Rush of blood to the head while lying down, [8] . ► Lightness in head, [12] . ► Lightness (of head) after nausea, [11] . ► Dulness (of head), [11] . ► Have had all day a singular sensation in head (third day), [7] . ► Feels as if a leaden ball was rolling about in the brain, [5] . ► Pain in the head (fifth day), [7] . [60.] ► Slight pain in head and heart (fourth day), [4a] . ► Pain from mouth up through head, and down to back of neck, quite severe, [7b] . ► Pain in head, which was very severe till 9 P.M. (fourth day), [2a] . ► Intense pain in head, extending back of forehead to organ of firmness, at 2 P.M.; soon after to whole to of head, or to eyes, lasting all day (second day), [7] . ► Burning waving towards the head, [11] . ► After moving, turning, stooping, feels as if head would burst, [11] . ► Head feels as if it would spit, and a great pressure on top, [5] . ► Dull, heavy pain in the head, [3] . ► Headache all day (fourth day), [1a] ; (sixth day), [7] . ► Some headache and pain in eyes (sixth day), [7] . [70.] ► Walked out and had headache, or after walking five minutes felt sharp pain across the eyebrows or up the nose; felt exceedingly fatigued and weary after a short walk (fourth day), [7a] . ► Headache in the bones, [9] . ► Towards noon slight headache, [5] . ► At noon slight headache, lasting all day, [1a] . ► Headache severe, [7b] ; (fifth day), [7a] . ► No pains till 3 P.M., when head ached severely until 9 P.M., [5] . ► Headache quite severe all day, and implicating eyes; feel lazy, requires an effort to think (fourth day), [7] . ► Headache quite severe, [5] . ► At 3 P.M. headache very severe, [5] . ► A severe, impatient headache through the whole morning, [7b] . [80.] ► A very intolerable, snappish, irritable headache for three days, with the jaws stiff and sore, the hands numb (after nearly a month), [7b] . ► Headache alternately with flushes in face, [11] . ► Afternoon pain in head, sick headache, [7b] . ► Considerable pressing pain in head (seventh day), [7] . ► Tearing and shooting-like in the bone (of the head), [9] . ► Irritable headache, touching the head makes it ache (sixth day), [7a] . ► Throbbing headache in forehead, vertex, and occiput, down to the neck, [9] . ► Throbbing, pulsating headache, most severe in right temple and over right eye, [1c] . ► Headache all day, most severe in right temple, beating, throbbing, and pulsating, [1c] . ► A slow vascillation or waving of the head from losing something in the upper part of the head, [8] . [90.] ► Dulness in the middle of the brain where it waves and moves, [8] . ► Shocks (in head) like dizziness, [11] . ► Headache relieved by cold air, [7b] . ► Forehead. ► Dulness in the forehead, more to the right side, [8] . ► Pain in forehead, in the protuberances, soon, [10] . ► Pain in head, greatest one and a half inches above left eye, [7b] . ► Great pain in head, from temple to temple, all day, [4a] . ► Slight burning pain in forehead, [7a] . ► Dull pain in forehead, especially on the left side, with a stupid feeling, [10] . ► Dull, heavy pain in forehead, and sharp, pricking pain in the left temple, sometimes attended with throbbing and pinching, [2] . [100.] ► Severe, heavy, dull pain in forehead, lasting five hours, [2] . ► During the afternoon and evening, headache over the eyes, [9] . ► Head aches severely from temple to temple (fifth day), [5] . ► Pressive pain in forehead, [12] , [9] , [11] ; with sensation of heat, [11] . ► Pressive pain in forehead and vertex, worse after moving the head and stooping, [9] , [12] . ► Pressing pain on top of forehead, while reading and thinking, in the afternoon, with restlessness of mind, [12] . ► Violent pressing outwards in forehead; the patient put the head towards the wall, [16] . [authority, Behlert, erase "16."] ► At 9 P.M., severe shooting pains in the head over the eyes, and in the temples, also a very violent, aching pain inside of an all over the chest (second day), [2a] . ► Severe headache from temple to temple, of a throbbing, beating nature (fifth day), [4] . ► Temples. ► Pain in the temples from the jaws, [11] . [110.] ► During the day, headache very intense at times in right temple, [7b] . ► At 9 A.M., most severe headache in both temples and over eyes unbearable, causing him to cry bitterly, and refuse to take another dose, [3] . ► Boring in temples every second day, in the left, morning (fourth day), [12] . ► Boring in the right temple every second day; in the left in the morning (fourth day), [12] . ► Vertex. ► Could detect no symptoms, save a slight headache on top (eighth day), [7a] . ► Much pressing on top of head; feels as if a form, fitting top of head, was pressing on it (fifth day), [7] . ► Pressing pain on top (of head) and forehead, while reading and thinking, in the afternoon, with restlessness of mind, [12] . ► Painful pressure on top, by moving the head, with fever and prostration, [12] . ► Pressing heaviness on top and to the right (of head), [9] . ► Pressing shutting on top of head, [8] . [120.] ► Beating, pressing on top; painful pressure on top (of head) on moving the head, with fever and prostration, [12] . ► Strange pulsation on top of head, [11] . ► Parietals. ► Numbness in left side of head, [11] . ► Right side of head feels stiff, as if it would become numb (third day), [7] . ► Pressure in left side of head, with boring and shooting inwards, extends to forehead and eyes, in the evening, [12] . ► Tearing from forehead towards left side, [9] . ► Left side of head is now, and has always been, most severely affected (sixth day), [7] . ► Occiput. ► Dulness in occiput, after awhile becoming painful, [8] . ► Slight pain in head, back part (seventh day), [7] . ► Pressing on occiput, upper side, [9] . ► External Head. [130.] ► My hair, which is usually dry, has become very oily to-day, the first time I remember such an occurrence (third day), [2] . ► Skin of head seems contracted and pinched up (fifth day), [7a] . ► Scalp very sensitive, [2a] . ► Whenever I put my finger on my head, there is always a strong subsequent pain, lasting a considerable time, [7b] . ► Itching in "acquisitiveness" in the morning (fifth day), [7] . ► On right side of vertex something comes from within and itches, [8] . EYE. ► Objective. ► Eyes somewhat red and inflamed (the cornea), [1c] . ► No pain, but eyes still slightly red, and occasional stitches in right temple, [3] . ► Eyes bloodshot and painful (fourth day), [3] . ► Inflammation in eyes during the day (tenth day), [7] . [140.] ► Inflammation of one eye; frothy matter pours out between the lids; pimples around the eyes since the bite of a dog in the finger, [16] . [A cure. -BRAUNS.] ► Subjective. ► Weakness of eyes when looking upwards, [9] . ► Great weakness in eyes, but no pain, [5] . ► Some pain in eyes (eighth day), [7] . ► Burning in eyes, [9] . ► Headache not severe; pain extends into right eye (fourth day), [2] . ► Eyes ached intensely, [7b] . ► Soreness in eyes (fourth day), [4] . ► Soreness in eyes, and pain in forehead above them (second day), [4] . ► Soreness in eyes and throat, and difficulty in swallowing (fourth day), [40] . [150.] ► Drawing, beating pain over the eyes and into the balls, [10] . ► A curious stinging pain in left eye, extending to forehead, over right eye painful, at 9 A.M. (third day), [2] . ► Tickling and heat in eyes, [11] ; from the neck, [11] . ► Brow. ► Pain over the left eye previous to retiring (fourth day), [7a] . ► Pain in small spot over right eyebrow, rendered worse by writing, [7b] . ► Pain over right eye inwards, pressing the temple, [9] . ► Sharp pain across eyebrows, afterwards burning in the eyelids (fifth day), [7a] . ► Drawing-beating pain over the eyes and into the balls, [10] . ► Lids. ► Eyelids as if they were lame, or were firmly closed, in the morning, [9] . [By Behlert, erase "9."] ► Burning in the lids, [11] . [160.] ► Burning in the eyeballs, [10] . ► Vision. ► Left eye extremely sensitive to light, and weeps, unusual with me, my eyes being very strong, [7b] . ► Dimness of sight, with giddiness, [9] . ► Something moves before the eyes, but always at a distance from the place she looks at, [9] . EAR. ► Rush of blood to right ear, after it a pressure, as if from a dull point, [8] . ► Ears feel very still, [7a] . ► Buzzing in right ear, [12] . ► Rushing noise, like water falling, in left ear, [9] . NOSE. ► Objective. ► Frequent sneezing, ceases after his attention is called to it, [14] . ► Often coagulated blood in the nose, [9] . ► Subjective. [170.] ► Pain in nose (second day), [7a] . ► Pain in the nose and right neck and side; feel very stiff, most about the jaws (fifth day), [7] . ► Nose pains from thinking, [11] . ► Headache extends into the nose, [11] . ► Sharp pain runs up the nose, [11] . ► Nose feels bruised (fifth day), [7a] . ► Tickling in nose, [11] . ► Itching in nose (all day), [7] . ► Smell. ► My sense of smell, which is always extremely acute, became painfully so, particularly in reference to unpleasant effluvia; the action of the nostrils was extremely painful, [7b] . ► The greatest sensibility to the smell of tobacco; tastes the snuff, while the box is one foot distant, [7c] . FACE. ► Objective. [180.] ► Paleness and qualmishness, [9] . ► Quivering in the face, [11] . ► Slight twitchings in face and hands, [7b] . ► Face pale yellow, nearly brown, [8] . ► Subjective. ► Heat in the face after drinking coffee, [9] . ► Heat in face, with redness, [9] . ► Heat in face, and soreness of left cheek, from thinking, [11] . ► It draws about in right side of face, [9] . ► Cheeks. ► Quick drawing from the right malar bone towards nose, [12] . ► Feeling as if bitten in the face, left side of mouth, [7] . [190.] ► Something seems gnawing right cheek-bone (fifth day), [7] . ► Rending pain in right upper jaw towards the ear; jaws ache with the headache, [11] . ► Shooting pains in upper jaw, [7a] . ► Chin. ► Biting-snapping, with convulsions, [11] . ► Several severe twitches in jaws, [7b] . ► Jaws feel stiff, and a disposition to gape, [7b] . ► Jaws stiff and sore, with headache (after nearly a month), [7b] . ► Both jaws feel stiff; tingling in cheek-bones (third day), [7] . ► Jaw quite stiff at times, more painfully so than before (tenth day), [7] . ► Jawache nearly all day, and pressure on top of head most severe, [7a] . [200.] ► While reading, jaws began to ache; the longer I read, the more they ached; gaped frequently (seventh day), [7] . ► It seems to make the jaws ache to write (second day), [7a] . ► Jaws ache severely (second day), [7a] . ► Jaws feel sore and stiff; have a great disposition to press the hand against the lower jaw, [7] . ► Jawbone feels quite sore, [7b] . MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► Teeth feel on edge (second day), [7a] . ► Pain in roots of back teeth, [9] . ► All the teeth ache, [9] . ► Headache and earache extend into the teeth, [11] . ► Teeth very sensitive; pulsation in teeth of upper jaw, [7b] . [210.] ► Toothache during pregnancy, [8] . ► Gums. ► Gums painful, [9] . ► Tongue. ► Pain in the root of the tongue and left side of throat (second day), [7a] . ► General Mouth. ► Feeling of coolness in mouth, as after peppermint, [14] . ► Felt as if the velum pendulum palati was elongated; examining, found it not so, but was slightly inflamed (fifth day), [1] . ► Have had quite a sore mouth, feels as if there were lumps in it, [7b] . ► Have had, for a week previous to taking medicine, a dry, parched mouth, with great thirst; now, mouth full of saliva, and total disinclination to drink (third day), [7] . ► Saliva. ► Increase of saliva, not more viscid than usual (third day), [1a] . ► Towards noon, slight increased of saliva, [5] . ► Much saliva, [6a] . [220.] ► Much saliva, not thicker than ordinary (sixth day), [7] . ► Great flow of saliva and difficulty in swallowing liquids (second day), [4a] . ► Saliva more plentiful, but thin, and of a yellow color (fifth day), [2] . ► Much viscid saliva (second day), [2] , [1c] . ► A large quantity of viscid saliva in mouth, causing me to spit an unusual quantity (third day), [2] . ► A very large amount of viscid saliva, and an increase of oil in the hair (fourth day), [2] . ► Saliva more viscid, constant spitting, feeling of general malaise all over, without pain, or knowing where to locate it (fourth day), [1] . ► Saliva very viscid, not more copious than usual, [3] . ► Saliva very viscid and plentiful, sore throat all day (third day), [2] . ► A great flow of spittle (third day), [4] . [230.] ► A great flow of mucous spittle (second day), [4] . ► Much tenacious mucus in mouth and throat, [1b] . ► Taste. ► All through this proving, could not get my food to taste of salt, though I used more than usual, [7b] . THROAT. ► Objective. ► Inflammation of the throat (Braung). ► Slight inflammation of throat and fauces as far as I could see (third day), [1a] . ► Throat much inflamed, some difficulty in swallowing, [1c] . ► Much mucus in throat and nose (fourth day), [7a] . ► During the day, bile rises in the throat; an unusual quantity of tough saliva in mouth and throat, [7] . ► A terrible pain in throat all day, and great pain in swallowing, [5] . ► Great heat in the throat and around the heart (second day), [1a] . [240.] ► Throat feels as though I had swallowed a small quantity of red pepper (fourth day), [1] . ► At 3 P.M., felt a strange constrictive sensation in back part of throat never experienced before; at 4 P.M., worse, could not swallow without great pain; went off at 6 P.M.; returned in an hour, lasting till 10 P.M., [2] . ► Violent spasms of throat at 2 P.M., feeling as if I was about to be suffocated; went off at 9 P.M., [1c] . ► Sore throat, [3] , [4a] . ► Sore throat all day (fourth day), [1a] , [3] . ► Throat feels sore and as if swelled, [6a] . ► Sore throat, increased saliva, no inflammation, as far I could see, though it felt as if inflamed, and hotter than usual (seventh day), [1a] . ► Sore throat, constant desire to swallow, [6a] . ► Very slight sore throat, constant desire to swallow (second day), [1] . ► Sore throat increased, slight difficulty in swallowing liquids, much saliva, rather more viscid than usual (third day), [1] . [250.] ► Sore throat, not able to swallow without great pain (fifth day), [5] . ► Sore throat and headache all day, [1b] . ► Slight sore throat, difficulty in swallowing liquids; this time a real difficulty; epiglottis appeared partially paralyzed (this symptom continued three days in several persons, going off by degrees); no difficulty in swallowing solids, [1a] . ► Slight sore throat, [1b] , [7] . ► Throat quite sore, headache in both temples, numbness in both arms, slight pain in lumbar region all day, [1b] . ► Quite a sore throat, constrictive sensation much worse when attempting to swallow liquids, which I could not do without pain; solids not painful, [2] . ► Violent sore throat, quite inflamed, could not swallow, liquids ran through the nose, [1c] . ► Very violent sore throat; soreness over whole body, as if beaten, [6a] . ► Soreness in throat from 11 A.M. till noon, [3] . ► Soreness and suffocation in back of throat at 10 A.M., [4] . [260.] ► Throat feels raw and as if about to become very sore (fifth day), [7] . ► Burning down in the sophagus, [8] . ► A desire to swallow, and spittle more copious and viscid than usual, [1] . ► Constant desire to swallow (third day), [1a] ; (sixth day), [7] . ► Constant effort to swallow, or dislodge mucus, which seems glued between nose and throat (eighth day), [7] . ► Feeling of suffocation has left the throat, but still I have difficulty in swallowing, also soreness in the eyes, but not the pain above them (third day), [4] . ► Slight difficulty in swallowing, [1a] . ► Great difficulty in swallowing; sore throat (second day), [2a] . ► Just before taking first morning dose, felt as if I was unable to swallow; tried a mouthful of cold water, swallowed it readily, but with a kind of aching in throat, followed by a slight burning; this burning lasted all day (fourth day), [1] . ► Feel as if I was going to have the mumps (second day), [7a] . [270.] ► Rending and tearing pain in right jugular bone, [9] . ► Gnawing in right jugular bone, [7] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Better appetite than usual, [7a] . ► Appetite to-day and yesterday perfectly ravenous, [7b] . ► Likes cold apple-butter, [8] . ► Little appetite in the morning, [8] . ► Aversion and disgust to everything fat, particularly mutton, [8] . ► Thirst. ► Not thirty these two weeks, [7a] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea, with pale face, after diarrha, [11] . ► Sickness of the stomach after eating eggs or fat meat; nausea, with giddiness and headache, [11] . [280.] ► Vomiting of the supper at night, [11] . ► Stomach. ► Noise in the stomach like bubbles of water; pressure like something pointing, [8] . ► Pressing inwards in pit of stomach after eating, [9] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. ► Pressing pain in hypochondriac regions after quick walking, [12] . ► Painful throbbing, like an abscess, in the hypochondriac regions, [9] . ► Gnawing pressure in the left hypochondriac region, [8] . ► Tearing from left (hypochondriac region) to right, [9] . ► Pain in region of liver and right kidney, [7c] . ► General Abdomen. ► Difficult and impeded passage of wind, [8] , [11] . ► Burning waving and rolling (in abdomen), [8] . [290.] ► Feeling of coldness and burning in abdomen, [9] . ► Violent bellyache, [17] . ► Pressive drawing and wrenching sensations (in abdomen), [9] . ► Shooting in abdomen, [9] , [11] , [17] . ► Shooting in right side of abdomen, [9] . ► Iliac Region. ► Glands in groins swelled very much and pained greatly for two hours (sixth day); not much abated (seventh day), [2] . ► Pain in both groins; the right groin has two little balls swelled up under the skin, quite painful (fifth day), [4] . ► Much pain in right groin and slight swelling there at 2 P.M. (fifth day), [7a] . ► Dull pressure above the right groin, on a small, distinct point, [12] . ► A dragging pain in both groins, [2] . RECTUM AND ANUS. [300.] ► Piles protrude, [11] . ► Blood from anus during menstruation, [11] . ► Bright-red blood from anus, with a horrible burning and stinging, like thorns, [6] . ► Sphincter (ani) closes violently, [11] . ► Pulsation outside of anus, [11] . STOOL. ► Diarrha, [9] , [18] . ► Diarrha, without pain, in the morning, [8] ; with pain in the morning, [7c] . ► Softer stool than usual, [8] , [9] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder. ► Cold and burning feeling in bladder; pain in sphincter, [8] . ► Urethra. ► After urinating, flow of prostatic juice, [8] . [310.] ► Burning and tenesmus in region of prostate and down urethra, [10] . ► Tickling burning in urethra after urination, [12] . ► Micturition. ► Urging to urinate, [8] . ► Very frequent urination; very copious watery urine, [10] . ► Urine. ► More urine and brighter colored, [8] . ► Urine brown, turbid, with reddish or whitish sediment, [8] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Male. ► Great weakness of the male parts; they feel empty, as if gone; bearing down in urethra, [8] . ► Tickling and burning on corona glandis, with some greenish pus, [10] . ► Aching in testicles, [8] . ► Lascivious ideas and erection, afternoon, [8] . [320.] ► Indifference during erection, and even during coition, [8] . ► Nightly emission in sleep, with dreams, [10] . ► No sperm during coition; the sperm passed off in sleep, [8] . ► Female. ► During the day, great pain, extending from uterus into breast and right side of abdomen (seventh day), [7] . ► Bearing down in uterine region, [9] . ► Sharp pains in uterus, shooting down to labia, [7c] . ► Slimy leucorrha during the proving, [7c] . ► Menstruation after fifty days, with headache, [7c] . ► Menstruation after two weeks, with hæmorrhoids, pulsation in anus, and weakness of back, [9] . ► Taken three days after menstruation is over, it reappears in two cases, [9] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. [330.] ► Sighing, [11] . ► Sighing, with oppression of breathing, [11] . ► Sighing, with pain in heart, [11] . CHEST. ► Rush of blood from the chest upwards, with toothache during pregnancy, [8] . ► Pain from within outwards, in right side, [11] . ► Pain in left side, [7a] . ► Dull pricking pain in left side from head to waist, [3] . HEART. ► Slight pain in heart and head (fourth day), [4a] . BACK. ► Pain in back and both groins, [5] . ► Aching in loins into back, [11] . [340.] ► Aching from loins down to feet, [7a] . ► Drawing downwards (in loins), [8] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Severe pain in all the joints, [5] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Numbness in both arms, [1] . ► Hands numb, with the headache (after nearly a month), [7b] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Heavy bruised feeling in both thighs, [2] . ► Legs felt heavy and throat sore, [5] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. ► Chlorosis (Barr, Gross). ► Great weakness and prostration, [8] . ► Nervous and irritable (seventh day), [7a] . [350.] ► Felt very nervous and much excited (third day), [7b] . ► Feel lazy, requires an effort to think (fourth day), [7] . ► General malaise all over, without pain, or knowing where to locate it (fourth day), [1] . ► Feel very bad to-day, severe pain in eyes, and in all my joints, [5] . SKIN. ► Pimples on left forehead, afterwards on the right, [12] . ► Slow maturing, bluish pimples on face, [8] . ► Trickling in the left cheek, at 6 A.M., [7] . ► Itching in root of penis, [8] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Excited, cannot sleep, [11] . ► Admires his own skill in talking fluently Latin in a dream, [8] . [360.] ► Subordination, like a servant in a dream, [14] . FEVER. ► Flushes (in the face) and headache alternately, [9] . ► Face sweats, with flushes, [11] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), Eyelids as if lame; little appetite. ► ( Afternoon ), While reading and thinking, pain on top of head . ► ( Evening ), At 9 o'clock, pains over eyes, etc. ► ( After coffee ), Heat in face. ► ( When looking upward ), Weakness of eyes. ► ( Moving ), Bursting feeling in head. ► ( Stooping ), Vertigo; bursting feeling in head. ► ( Writing ), Pain over eye. ► Amelioration. ► ( Cold air ), Headache. ► ( After meals ), Feels better. Clinical: Bubo; Clairvoyance; Convulsions; Corns, pains in; Diarrhœa; Dysentery; Fever; Hair, oiliness of; Headache; Hydrophobia; Hypersensitiveness; Landry's paralysis; Leucorrhœa; Lyssophobia; Mania; Nervousness; Neuralgia. Œsophagus, stricture of; Paralysis; Pregnancy, convulsions of; toothache of; Respiratory paralysis; Salivation; Satyriasis; Sciatica; Sunstroke; Tetanus; Ulcers; Uterus, prolapse of; Vaginismus; Wounds, too rapid healing of

Voice altered in tone; tones much suppressed; hoarse; rough; harsh and weak (last stage); shrill, inarticulate sounds; shrill sounds of utmost despair, or occasioned by violent expirations; very shrill and piercing bark, changing near its termination into a distressing, continuous howl (dogs). Epiglottis crisp and dry (R.). Sighing, with pain in heart. Dyspnœa: with flatulency, cough and rattling in Chest; with sighing, groaning respiration; from cardiac pain; lying down. Oppression in breathing before a severe suffocative attack, induced by spasmodic contractions of respiratory muscles, combined with spasmodic, alarming constriction of pharynx. Breathing during paroxysm gasping, irregular and usually quite rapid, often with decided dyspnœa. Breathing peculiar; held for a time, and then a few rapid breaths (P.). Frothed at the mouth very much; almost choked with it (P.). Weight and heaviness of legs and shoulders. Severe twitching in arms and legs, much resembling chorea. Cramp in arms. The pain up arm was followed by cramps and drawing in back and limbs of bitten side. Weakness in arms. Right arm becomes so heavy and inactive that writing is too great an exertion, and he allows arm to drop. Hand trembles so much he can scarcely write. Hands numb, with headache. Feeling as if hip bones would slip out of their sockets, > by resting hands on hips. A pressive pain in r. hip bone, goes from there to middle of sacral bones. L. hip aches in bone. Along l. sciatic nerve a dull pain, returning periodically; when rising from sitting. Twitching in legs. Weakness in legs when going upstairs. Each dose he had taken made him feel as if he was getting corns on every toe, his real corns felt remarkably well and did not rain him at all. Drawing from neck to forehead, immediately followed by sparks before eyes and vanishing of sight; red face; involuntary grinding of teeth; second attack; the first was felt in head after washing in morning; seven days after bite of mad dog (Bellad., three doses, Hyos. interpolated once a day). Acute ascending paralysis (Landry's disease) diagnosed in early stage (P.). Bitten by a mad dog in sixth year in several places; became somnambulistic ten years after. Severe nervous twitches in whole body all day. Twitching of muscles throughout entire body (R.). Twitching of tendons with tendency to general convulsions (R.). Prostration beyond description (P.). Twitching every few minutes; sometimes more violently than others; most marked in abdominal walls (P.). Makes a peculiar noise like a waggoner driving horses (P.).

keynotes.text: Hering was the first (1833) to prove, and to suggest the employment of this nosode in medicine; and of late years it has become notorious through the experiments of Pasteur. Pasteur's method of administration is very different from that employed by homœopaths, but he is working on homœopathic lines in seeking to neutralise a virus in the system by introducing a modification of the same virus. His experiments led him to produce the rabic poison in a highly intensified form in the spinal cords of rabbits. He then modified its intensity in different degrees by exposure to air for a longer or shorter period. Patients who come to the Institute are inoculated first with the least potent, and later with the most potent "vaccin," after which they are pronounced "cured." The "cure" is, however, extremely uncertain, as the degree of susceptibility to the poison is unknown in any case, and many hundreds of the patients subjected to the inoculations have died of the disease. Pasteur's first method was admitted to be too strong, and was soon modified; a number of patients having died from the inoculations. One of these cases I investigated, and the symptoms were sufficiently striking to deserve recording. The patient was Arthur Wilde, of Rotherham, aged 29, and I received the account from his mother, who nursed him through his illness. He had been bitten severely by a man suffering from hydrophobia, and was persuaded, much against his wish, to go to Pasteur. This he did a few days after the bite, returning on October 19, 1886, after undergoing the course. On Saturday, October 30, he complained of a pricking sensation below the ribs in the right side, in the part where the injections had been made. Pressure relieved the pain somewhat. That evening he vomited, and the vomiting continued, and he became very prostrate. On Monday the prostration was intense, vomiting continued; restless; skin cool, perspiring; quite conscious. The spots where the inoculations were made were dark and livid. Twitching occurred every few hours, sometimes more violently than others; most marked on the abdomen. From Monday through Tuesday he was making a peculiar loud noise, something like a waggoner driving horses, "bis" "whoo," though he had never had to do with horses. He seemed completely helpless. On Tuesday night vomiting ceased and he began to froth a great deal. Early on Wednesday morning he began to talk thick. His breathing, which had been peculiar all through─he would hold his breath for a long time when making the noise and then breathe rapidly for a few breaths─became very bad at 3 a.m. on Wednesday. He died shortly after 12, having been apparently conscious to the end though unable to speak for the last hour. The frothing had increased up to the time of his death and he seemed to choke with it. This case was paralleled by that of Goffi, an attendant at St. Thomas' Hospital, who was bitten by a cat and sent to Pasteur. On his return he was taken ill, and his case was at first diagnosed as Landry's paralysis, but finally proved (by experiments made with his spinal cord) to be "paralytic rabies," the result of inoculation. It was after the occurrence of these and similar "accidents" that the intensity of the "vaccins" was reduced. It would be well to have as an alternative preparation, Hydrophobinum Pasteurianum, obtained from Pasteur's vaccin, to meet conditions similar to these. The pathogenesis of Hydrob. is made up partly of symptoms observed in rabid animals and human patients, but chiefly of symptoms developed in the provings. The remedy has been pretty extensively used in practice, not only in cases of hydrophobia, but in many disorders in which the keynote symptoms have been present. These are: Exquisite sensitiveness to breath of air; to bright objects, especially the surface of water; to sounds, and most of all to the sound of running water. Even the thought of it is enough to bring on an aggravation or a convulsion. Cases of dysentery with pain and tenesmus on hearing water running from a tap have been cured with Hydrob. Cannot bear heat of sun. Thirst, with inability to swallow. Copious viscid saliva. A great variety of mental disturbances occur. Rapid speech and impatience are very noticeable. The mental irritability is as great as that of the senses and dangerously violent temper is developed. The irritation is further shown in the sexual organs. Suffocating feelings were experienced by several persons. Sighing, and sighing respiration. The effects of bites of non-rabid dogs have been removed by this nosode. Hydrob. is a close analogue of many of the animal poisons, especially Laches., and I have no doubt when clinically developed it will prove to be equally important. Marked symptoms appear in every part of the body and mind. Many symptoms are by stooping; by motion generally. Sensitive to any changed position. Bending head backward > pain in neck. Throws head back when sneezing. Heat of sun , it is unbearable. Hot steam directed to bitten place > burning pain in it. > Turkish bath. Damp warm air oppressed him. Sensitive to least breath of air; air of agreeable temperature feels cold. Slightest draught Cold air > headache. From slightest touch; from riding in carriage. [In the Schema, the symptoms observed in cases of the disease are marked (R), those observed in Pasteur cases (P), the rest are the symptoms of the provings with some clinical additions.]

_raw: The remedies most closely related to Hydrob. are those which have cured cases of the disease─Bell., Stram., Hyo., Fagus, Agave, Laches., Canth., and the animal poisons generally. Lachesis is a very close ally ( from sun; bluish discoloration of wounds; irritability; warm, damp air; from touch and pressure; though the late evolution of the symptoms of Hdphb. contrasts with the lightning-like rapidity of snake-venom effects). Compare also: in ascending paralysis, Gels., Con.; in respiratory paralysis, Solania, Bell., Dulc.; in sexual excitement, Canth., Pic. ac., Graph.; in intolerance of sun, Gels., Glon., Nat., Lach., Apis; in effects of carriage-riding, Coccul.; desire to urinate on seeing running water, Canth., Sul.; convulsions from dazzling light, Stram.; consciousness of womb, Helon.; viscid saliva, Epipheg., Hydras.; hurried speech, Hyo.; in coldness, Helod. It follows well: Tabac. (headache); Arg. n. (uterine disease); Stram. (neuralgia). Is followed well by: Nat. m.

mental_emotional.text: Loss of consciousness sometimes at an early stage, but not generally until a short time before death (R.). Does not hear or see persons around him (R.). Memory for single words much improved. Thoughts of something terrible going to happen come into his mind against his will; feels impelled to do reckless things, such as throwing a child, which he carries in his arms, through the window. They appreciate the formidable character of the disease and speak frequently with a remarkably quick and sharp articulation of the impending fatal results (R.). During the tranquil intervals, responded correctly to questions put to him, recognised those around him, and with a presentiment of impending death, begged them to pray for him and not leave him alone (R.). Most commonly the mental faculties are in a superior state of excitement, shown by quick perception, amazing acuteness of understanding and rapidity with which they answer questions (R.). It seems to her as if two entirely different trains of thought influenced her at the same time. During convulsions, mental illusions and hallucinations; in intervals of consciousness mental faculties are retained (R.). Imagine that they are abused, and energetically defend themselves against attacks and insults, which in reality are products of their own fancy (R.). Fancies he is blown at by several persons, some of whom are not present (R.). Thinks he is a dog or a bird, and runs up and down, chirping and twittering, until he falls down fainting (R.). Strange notions and apprehensions during pregnancy. Insane ideas enter his head; for instance, to throw a glass of water, which he is carrying in his hand, into some one's face, or to stab his flesh with the knife he is holding, and the like. (Mania spermatica in stallions.). Inclination to be rude and abusive, to bite and strike. A strong and uncontrollable impulse to do certain acts; to spring at and bite any moving object that came within reach; dog (R.). Lament with great anxiety their inability to relieve thirst which afflicts, and by various contrivances endeavour eagerly to drink (R.). Break out of their stables furiously and run or jump over ditches and fences (sheep). Not afraid of dogs, but dislikes to see them because their sight renews her fear (lyssophobia, after bite by non-rabid dog). Exhilarated, felt as if he had received joyful intelligence. Ill-humour. Hypersensitiveness of all the senses. On a watch held to scrobiculum he sees the hour and minute hands (R.). He says he can see hands on dial plate of church clock (R.). He could hear what was spoken in next room, and counted coppers in a room below him (R.). Linen dipped in sugar water, put on pit of stomach, gives a sweet taste in mouth (R.). Copper, if in his room, makes him restless and full of pains (R.). Sometimes he would control inclination to stool by a strong effort of will, but effort caused much nervous irritation. Attacks of nervous headache become awful and insupportable if he hears water run out of a hydrant. When he hears water poured out, or if he hears it run, or if he sees it, he becomes very irritable, nervous; it causes desire for stool and other ailments. The mere sight of a drinking vessel containing water is intolerable; they turn away their faces, shriek out loud, beckon anxiously with hands to have water removed, for voice and breath fail (R.). Thinking of fluids of any kind, even of blood, brings on convulsions.

head.text: Peculiar lightness in head; lightness after nausea. A slow vacillation or wavering of the head, from something being loose in upper part of head. Rush of blood to head: while lying down; from chest upward, with toothache; during pregnancy; when rising. Maddening outward pressing pain in forehead; he presses his head against the wall. Beating, throbbing headache; most severe in r. temple and above r. eye; each bone feels shattered and sore; from temple to temple. Violent headache, most in temples and forehead, during day and from stooping and stirring about. Frequent pressure on vertex, as if a cast, which fitted top of head, was pressing it down. Headaches from bites of dogs, rabid or not. In rare cases serous effusion in opaque subarachnoid tissue and lateral ventricle, and also increased adherence of membranes of brain to convolutions (R.). At noon slight headache, lasting all day. Burning aching from l. side of occiput down neck. Violent headache and backache. Irritable headache, touching head makes it ache; very sensitive scalp. Hair which is usually dry has become very oily. Scalp feels contracted and pinched.

eyes.text: Sensitive to light. Sight of water-agitation; renews idea of pain; causes convulsions (pregnancy). False vision, dulness of sight, together with dilatation of pupils, sometimes actual blindness. Vision much impaired or absent; lasts twelve hours. Clairvoyance. Pain in small spot over r. eyebrow, writing. Eyes are wild, rolling, staring and livid (R.). Swelling of eyelids after bite of dogs (cured in sheep). Extreme ulceration of eye, lids closed and puffed up by pus (in sheep).

ears.text: Conversation in vicinity of patient may throw him into a most violent agitation (R.). Hearing water poured out in next room makes him very irritable and nervous. Clairaudient.

nose.text: Strong odours may start spasms. The greatest sensibility to smell of tobacco; tastes snuff while box is one foot distant. Frequent sneezing, mostly early in morning or late in evening, as if a coryza would begin; also when looking at something bright, and from every little dust.

face.text: Both jaws feel stiff; tingling in cheek-bones. Gnawing and crawling sensation in (r.) zygoma. Facial muscles become variously contorted, countenance changes its aspect frequently. Face sweat: with sensation of heat; with flushes. The jawbones feel sore; aching in lower jaw. Masseter muscles not affected by spasms. Lower jaw stiff and painful; with inclination to yawn; with headache; imagines he cannot open his mouth. Spasms with froth before mouth (R.).

mouth_throat.text: Grinding of teeth. Toothache and other complaints during pregnancy, with internal ebullition of blood from chest to head; head feels as if filled with air to bursting. Difficult, incorrect speech (stricture of throat). Tongue coated with foam (R.). Pricking sensation under tongue. Ranula returns periodically, with dryness of mouth, in afternoon, soreness when chewing; with hæmorrhoids and constipation. Feeling of coldness in mouth, like essence of peppermint. Severe pain passing from mouth upward through head and down into neck. Tough, short frothy phlegm in mouth (horse). When vomiting ceased, frothing at the mouth began and was so excessive as nearly to choke him (P.). Frothed at mouth, attempted to spit out with much difficulty (before death). Saliva more viscid, constant spitting, feeling of general malaise. Entire mucous membrane of mouth and pharynx was of an equally distributed pink without any swelling. Slight redness of palate and throat, with spasm of œsophagus and difficult speech. Sore throat, as after swallowing red pepper. Cooling sensation in œsophagus. Sore throat, constant desire to swallow; much saliva and feeling as if beaten. Periodical spasm of œsophagus, continual painful inclination to swallow without being able to swallow anything; constriction is most severe when taking water into mouth, if he tried to swallow it forcibly, he had burning and stinging in the throat, cough and retching which forced fluid from his mouth; difficult speech.

stomach_abdomen.text: Voracious appetite; swallowed wheat without chewing. Excessive desire for salt. Abnormal cravings during pregnancy. Aversion to fat food and drink; there remains a long greasy aftertaste, after mutton. Warm drinks, such as milk, soups and wine, are more easily taken than water. Inability to take solid food, or else it is consumed with greatest difficulty. Nausea: with giddiness, headache and pale face after diarrhœa; food does not taste right; and loss of appetite in evening; 10 to 11 p.m. Gagging when he forcibly attempts to swallow water, forces it out of his mouth. Nausea and vomiting after diarrhœa. Vomiting of food; of fluid while drinking, followed by faintness; of what was eaten at supper, at night in sleep. Vomiting throughout three days, with prostration and restlessness; when vomiting ceased frothing began and nearly choked him (P.). Great oppression in stomach, has to open her clothes. A pressing pain: in r. side, near last ribs, with breathing; in hypochondria, after quick walking. Painful throbbing as if an abscess was forming in region of spleen, but very deep in, exact locality is half-way between median line and outline of l. side; it lasted eight days; with it departed remnant of a similar affection in this locality, against which eleven years of allopathic treatment had proved of no avail. Tearing from l. hypochondriac region to r. General soreness in whole of lower abdomen. Rigidity of muscles of abdomen. Pain in both groins; in r., two small kernels under skin, very painful. Inguinal glands very much swollen, they pain for two hours.

stool_rectum.text: Tenesmus during and after stool. Dysenteric stools with tenesmus; renewed as soon as he hears or sees water run. When in the morning some water was poured out from pitcher into basin, pain and desire to stool returned. Diarrhœa: with much pain, most during day, eighteen hours after dose, lasting twenty-four hours, with pain in lower part of bowels; in morning; followed by nausea as if she would have to vomit; attended with violent pains early in morning; after stitches in side. Stools of bloody mucus. Involuntary stools.

urinary.text: Urine too scanty and high-coloured (cured in a case of camp diarrhœa). Constant desire to urinate on seeing running water; urinates a little at a time. Prostatic juice passes after urinating.

male.text: Lasciviousness: after eating, with feeling of weakness in parts; with erections in afternoon. Strong erections, without sexual excitement or thoughts, in evening, while undressing in a cold room. Sexual indifference with erections, even during act of coition, which is perfectly performed. Increased sexual desire (dropsy of spine with sheep; hydrophobia of sheep). Priapism, with frequent seminal emissions. Satyriasis in a stallion; hot breath streamed from nostrils. Semen is discharged too late or not at all during coition. No emission during coition, but afterwards semen escaped unconsciously in sleep. Glans is dry and sticks to foreskin. Hydrocele. Atrophy of testicles; testicles diminish in size, first l. then r. Complaints resulting from abnormal sexual desire.

female.text: Pain in l. ovarian region, uneasiness there. Increase of uterine sensitiveness, conscious of having a womb. With a painful sensitiveness of womb, slight degree of prolapsus, so that after any considerable physical effort there would be a strong conviction that it was prolapsed. Any change of position that would tilt or rotate os uteri would cause much pain. Prolapsus uteri of seven years' standing. Severe leucorrhœa, with pains in back and lower part of bowels, sore vagina. Sensitiveness of vagina rendering coition quite painful. Weakness in back, with copious catamenia. During pregnancy: strange notions, desires or cravings; rush of blood from chest upward; toothache, backache and other complaints; great sense of bearing down; intense pain from inflammation of os and cervix (formerly treated with caustic); great soreness in lower part of back and bowels. Spasms excited whenever she attempts to drink water, or if she hears it poured from one vessel into another; sight or sound of water affects unpleasantly, even though desiring water (puerperal convulsions). Since cessation of lochia a severe leucorrhœa; pain in back and lower part of bowels; soreness of vagina. Both breasts swollen when waking in morning, she can hardly get up; three mornings in succession; same swelling of breasts at night when opening her dress.

chest_heart.text: Chest and abdomen feel expanded; expanding chest seems to invigorate him, though it usually fatigues. Stitches in heart from ringing of church bells. Stitches in heart, when walking; they would kill him if they continued. Heart had for three months not been free from a sticking, drawing, squeezing pain, result of an attack of rheumatism and cold, together with a palpitation and difficulty of breathing. Violent pain in heart, as if it would burst or had needles running into it. Pain in cardiac region, to which he is subject, is half an hour after the dose, but much > in several days. Heart palpitated violently and felt as if it was coming up into throat; drank several mouthfuls of water, which relieved.

back.text: Pressing in neck and up back of head. Backache and headache. Considerable pain in lower part of back, with soreness felt through to pubic region.

skin.text: Quick tendency of the wound to heal (the same in leprosy). Biting, itching in various parts of body, by scratching. Bluish discoloration of bitten place (after Laches). Pustules on forehead; around inflamed eye; on finger (after bite). Malignant ulcers from bite of a dog. Red scar from bite of a dog. Dark, livid-marks where inoculations were made; pricking sensation in them, causing him to continually press his side, which relieved temporarily (P.). Cancerous sores.

sleep.text: Inclination to yawn, with stiffness of lower jaw. Frequent yawning without sleepiness, particularly when hearing others yawn. Insomnia; sleepless in spite of narcotics (R.). Starting in sleep, afternoon. On waking is morose, inclined to be angry. In morning, after exciting dreams, much fatigued, feels tired in sacrum and back. On awaking from siesta numbness in head.

fever_chill.text: Paroxysms of intense coldness with pain in spine. Chilly feeling, more down r. (bitten) arm. Became cold in bed at 3 a.m., although covered with four blankets; lasted about one hour. Chills intermixed and followed by heat and cold sweat. Cannot bear heat of sun. Fever every evening, commencing at dusk and lasting until bedtime (midnight). He feels the pulse beat through the body; from time to time there is a surging through throat into head, like a slow wave. Sensation of heat felt internally and externally through entire body, no external warmth, it forces perspiration out on face as from weakness, and is accompanied by lassitude and aching in legs. Restless, skin cool, perspiring, quite conscious (P.). At 9 p.m. a dripping warm perspiration from whole r. hand, from wrist to nails; afterwards hands and fingers stiff, she can hardly bend them (R.). Skin covered with a clammy sweat (last stage) (R.). Skin moist, even covered with sweat; during spells, limbs cold and livid. Much better after perspiring. (A number of cures of hydrophobia have been reported from forced perspirations, and e. p. from prolonged and repeated use of the Turkish bath. This is known as the Buisson treatment, from Dr. Buisson who originated it.). Intermittent fever. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.
